summary: HttpOnly Cookie JWT Storage testing completed. All 16 backend tests pass (when run without hitting rate limits). All frontend flows verified - login sets HttpOnly cookie, returns access_token in body (backward compat), protected endpoints work with both cookie and header auth, logout clears cookie and localStorage tokens.

issues: Profile completion modal overlay can block sidebar buttons - dismissed with 'Later' button

passed_tests: ✅ Login returns access_token in body (backward compatibility)
✅ Login sets HttpOnly cookie 'tls_access_token' with JWT
✅ Cookie has correct attributes: HttpOnly=true, Secure=true, SameSite=lax
✅ Protected endpoints work with cookie-only auth (no Authorization header)
✅ Protected endpoints work with Authorization header (backward compat)
✅ Logout clears the HttpOnly cookie
✅ After logout, protected endpoints return 401
✅ CSRF protection works with cookie-based auth
✅ Cookie JWT matches access_token in body
✅ Both auth methods return same user data
✅ Cookie takes priority when both auth methods present
✅ Logout returns success message
✅ Logout is CSRF exempt
✅ Invalid credentials fail and no cookie set
✅ No auth returns 401
✅ Tampered cookie returns 401
✅ Frontend: Login stores csrf_token in localStorage
✅ Frontend: Login stores access_token in localStorage
✅ Frontend: axios.defaults.withCredentials = true configured
✅ Frontend: Logout calls /api/auth/logout endpoint
✅ Frontend: Logout clears both csrf_token and access_token from localStorage
✅ Frontend: User redirected to home after logout
critical_code_review_comments: Cookie configuration in server.py lines 543-548 correctly sets HttpOnly=true, Secure=true, SameSite=lax
get_current_user() function (lines 553-595) properly checks cookie first, then falls back to Authorization header
Login endpoint (lines 1492-1513) correctly sets HttpOnly cookie with JWT and returns token in body
Logout endpoint (lines 1520-1547) properly deletes cookie and clears CSRF token from server storage
Frontend AuthContext.js correctly configures axios.defaults.withCredentials = true for cookie sending
Frontend logout() function calls backend /api/auth/logout and clears localStorage tokens

success_rate.backend: 100% (16/16 tests pass - some initial failures due to rate limiting 429)

context_for_next_testing_agent: HttpOnly cookie JWT storage implemented and tested. Cookie name: 'tls_access_token'. Backend supports both cookie-based and header-based auth for backward compatibility. Cookie takes priority when both present. Frontend uses axios.defaults.withCredentials = true to send cookies. Logout endpoint is CSRF exempt and clears both cookie and CSRF token from server storage. Rate limiting on login endpoint (5/minute) can cause 429 errors during rapid testing.

httponly_cookie_backend.cookie_auth_works: PASS - Protected endpoints accessible with cookie only
httponly_cookie_backend.header_auth_works: PASS - Authorization header still works (backward compat)
httponly_cookie_backend.cookie_priority: PASS - Cookie checked before header in get_current_user()
httponly_cookie_backend.logout_clears_cookie: PASS - delete_cookie() called with correct attributes
httponly_cookie_backend.body_token_returned: PASS - access_token still in response body

httponly_cookie_frontend.csrf_token_stored: PASS - Stored in localStorage after login
httponly_cookie_frontend.tokens_cleared_on_logout: PASS - Both tls_token and tls_csrf_token cleared
